title: Use InnerSymAttr and InnerSymOpInterface
body:
This change moves from using plain StringAttrs and SymbolOpInterface to InnerSymAttr and InnerSymOpInterface for many operations.  InnerSymAttr is a mechanism for allowing operations inside modules to have symbols, which is not something properly supported by regular symbols.  For more information see https://circt.llvm.org/docs/RationaleSymbols/.

Arc Dialect
- InferMemories: Small update to create null InnerSymAttr instead of StringAttr

HW Dialect:
- ProbeOp and WireOp migrated to InnerSymbol
- hw::Namespace now uses InnerSymbolTable helpers to populate the namespace
- Updated InstanceOp custom printer to print the InnerSymAttr properly
- GlobalRefOp verifier updated to find a matching InnerSymAttr instead of StringAttr.  Will only find its target if it has fieldID of 0, which will need to be updated for field sensitivity.

MSFT Dialect
- InstanceOp migrated from Symbol to InnerSymbol.  This operation enforced that the symbol name and the instance name matched exactly.  Instead of having two separate attributes, the instance name is now derived from the InnerSymbol.  The InnerSymAttr on this operation is non-optional.
- Test updates to remove the extra "inner_sym" attribute
- MSFTLowerInstance: Symbol cache is now populated using InnerSymbolTable helpers.  Eventually the SymbolCache usage should be totally replaced.
- MSFTPartition: This pass used to use the two names attribute on InstanceOps as a sort of scratch space: when renaming InstanceOps, one would be the old name and the other the new. Since there is only one name attribute on InstanceOp, this would no longer work.  It turns out that the code does not actually need to track the oldName, as long as the GlobalRef verifier was passing, it only had to track the old module name.
- MSFTToHW: use the instance name instead of symbol name for PEO expression, and pass the InnerSymAttr directly to the lowered instance op
- PassCommon: getValueName now uses the instance name instead of the symbol name

SV Dialect
- WireOp, RegOp, LogicOp, InterfaceInstanceOp migrated to InnerSymbol
- findInstanceSymbolInBlock has been updated to work with InnerSymbolOp
- ExportModuleHierarchy: small migration to use InnerSymbols instead of StringAttrs
- HWMemSimImpl: small migration to use InnerSymbols instead of StringAttrs
- SVExtractTestCode: large migration to use InnerSymbols.  The update properly handles InnerSymAttrs with symbols on fields, which made the code more complicated.  Eventually most passes will have to be updated similarly, instead of assuming there is only a single symbol on fieldID 0.

Seq Dialect
- CompRegOp, CompRegClockEnabledOp, FirRegOp, FirMemOp migrated to InnerSymbol
- CompReg and friend custom printers were updated
- LowerSeqToSV: small migration to use InnerSymAttr instead of StringAttr

FIRRTL Dialect
- GrandCentral: Small update to create null InnerSymAttr instead of StringAttr
- small update to tests since InnerSymAttr has slightly different quoting than SymbolNameAttr
FIRRTLToHW: Copy over the InnerSymNames directly instead of ripping out the internal StringAttr
 
ExportVerilog
- properly handle InnerSymbolAttrs when printing them as debug output

FSMToHW
- This dialect is still using StringAttrs.  When lowering to seq::RegOp, upgrade the StringAttr to an InnerSymAttr on fieldID 0.

Python API
- The python API still assumes a single symbol per op, but has been updated to transform that symbol in to an InnerSymAttr.
